Off Calendar Jan-24-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jasmine H. Ng, Esq. of GATES GONTER GUY PROUDFOOT & MUENCH, LLP, T:949.753.0255, for cross-defendant/moving party;
Mikhail Mdinaradze, Esq. of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP, T:310.445.0800, for cross-complainant/opposing party;

Julian Wu, Esq. of ARMIJO & GARCIA, T:213.612.5330, for plaintiff Federal Insurance, on Court Call.

The Court provided the parties with a copy of his tentative ruling at the start of the hearing. The matter was passed to allow parties to read the tentative ruling.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court takes CROSS DEFENDANT CROWN ENERGY SERVICES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Continued To Jan-24-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of And Motion For Application To Continue Trial And Discovery Cut Off
Off Calendar Aug-29-2019 Text Ruling

Motion For Leave To File A X-Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jasmine Ng, Esq. 949-753-0255 of Gates Goneter Guy Proudfoot & Muench LLP for Cross Defendant through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT CROWN ENERGY SERVICES, INC.'s Motion For Leave To File A Cross-Complaint.

Cross-Defendant Crown Energy Services, Inc. dba Able Engineering Services' motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted. The court "shall grant" leave to file a cross-complaint "at any time during the course of the action." (Code Civ. Proc. 426.50; see Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99 ["A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrate where forfeiture would otherwise result."].) Defendant Chronomite Laboratories, Inc. has not shown that Crown is acting in bad faith. If Chronomite wishes to challenge the viability of the proposed cross-complaint, it may do so by way of demurrer or motion for summary judgment. (See Brown v. Ralphs Grocery Co. (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 824, 843 ["The 'better course of action' is to allow a plaintiff to amend the complaint 'and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings.' [Citation.] Thus, a defendant is not required to make its arguments about the sufficiency of the pleading in an opposition to a motion for leave to amend."].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
